_comment: Copy to convene_messages.json beside the bot to re-voice or translate convene's reminders. Keep every {placeholder} exactly as it appears; a template with an unknown placeholder is rejected and the built-in default kept. Placeholders: {ping} (role mention or empty), {title}, {when} (Discord absolute timestamp), {when_rel} (Discord relative timestamp — renders 'in 12 hours' and counts down live), {hours} (integer hours, if you prefer a plain number), {count}, {quorum}, {dm_mention}, {recorder_line}. The 'prep' template is the frame around a DM's /session prep ask: its only placeholders are {ping} and {body} (the DM's verbatim words) — keep both, and keep {body} on its own so the ask reads cleanly. The reveal digest fires when pages newly reach the projection: 'reveal' is the batch frame ({count} pages, {entries} the rendered list, {ping} available but off by default), and 'reveal_item' is one line per page ({title} and {link}, which is a newline + the published URL or empty). The 'event' template is the one neutral heads-up a non-session calendar entry gets when SESSION_MATCH carves sessions out by name keyword: its placeholders are {ping}, {title}, and {when} only — no quorum or session framing.
created: {ping}A session is on the calendar: **{title}**, {when}. React *interested* on the event so we can call quorum.
at_risk: Heads up{dm_mention} — **{title}** is {when_rel} and only {count} of {quorum} are in. Rally the table, or pick a new night while there's time.
imminent: {ping}**{title}** is on tonight — quorum met ({count}/{quorum}). {recorder_line}See you at the table.
ended: That's a wrap on **{title}**. When you get a moment: stage the recording and transcribe it, and the recap will announce itself once it's published.
prep:
{ping}A prep note for the table before next session:

{body}
reveal:
{ping}The veil lifts — {count} new page(s) in the archive:
{entries}
event: {ping}A new event is on the calendar: **{title}**, {when}.
